claims.claim: Abdul Hussein is an undersized bantamweight at 5 foot 5.
claims.quote: I think Abdullah Hussein just in general is a very undized bantamweight. This guy is 5 foot five and you can see it in his fights

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein is a bit small for the weight class and has trouble making 125 lbs.
claims.quote: I really wonder if there's any chance at all that this guy could make 125 lbs because I do think he's a bit small for this weight class.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein is small for the 135-pound weight class at 5 feet 5 inches tall.
claims.quote: this guy is small for this weight class. He's only 5'5

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is absolutely massive for the bantamweight division.
claims.quote: Cody Gibson who is absolutely massive for bantamweight. One of the biggest bantamweights maybe in the division.

origin.note: 4 channels cite the same public record — one origin, not 4 confirmations

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is taking the fight on short notice and traveling to Abu Dhabi.
claims.quote: Cody Gibson is taking this on short notice and also Cody Gibson not only needs to take the fight on short notice, make the weight on short notice, he also needs to go from the United States to Abu Dhabi

claims.claim: Cody Gibson has questionable cardio.
claims.quote: that's the cardio.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is prone to gassing out by the second round.
claims.quote: Gibson gas out of course in the second round

claims.claim: Cody Gibson was knocked out in 21 seconds in his last fight, raising some durability concerns.
claims.quote: he did get knocked out by Archie Long at only 21 seconds in his last one. So Mal maybe it could be time to worry about the chin.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is 38 years old and on a two-fight losing streak.
claims.quote: lost two in a row 38 years old kind of in that final phase

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is nearly 39 years of age with a record of 21 and 12.
claims.quote: Cody Gibson's almost 39 years of age, you know, he's 21 and 12

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is 38 years old and coming off two straight losses by finish.
claims.quote: 38 years old coming off two straight losses by finish and the last one was a quick KO loss to Archi Long.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is 38 years old and coming off a brutal first-minute knockout loss.
claims.quote: 38 years old at bantamweight coming off of a brutal knockout loss in the first minute.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is a very good wrestler who gets multiple takedowns and pushes a high striking pace.
claims.quote: Gibson is a very good wrestler. He does get multiple takedowns in the fights that he does win. he can put a very high pace on in the striking.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein has very bad takedown accuracy.
claims.quote: he does shoot a lot of takedowns, but he has a very, very bad takedown accuracy.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein has a high number of submission victories on his record.
claims.quote: he's got a lot of submission victories.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein possesses dangerous submission skills and strong ground and pound.
claims.quote: He's got nasty submission skills as well... Not only can he control you and beat you up with some nasty ground and pound, he is a very opportunist opportunistic submission guy

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein has a dangerous submission game and is an aggressive combination striker.
claims.quote: dangerous submission game, and definitely an aggressive combination striker

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein has a tendency to win fights by submission.
claims.quote: Abdul seems to win a lot by submission.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein has nine submissions and five knockouts as a finisher.
claims.quote: nine submissions, five knockouts

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein holds a violent knockout win over former UFC fighter Tyson Nam.
claims.quote: he has a win over former UFC fighter Tyson Nam. And it wasn't just a win. It was a violent knockout. Badly starched him.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein has a 15-2 professional record.
claims.quote: This is a 15-2 professional fighter.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein's most recent victory was a violent finish over Tyson Nam 6 months ago.
claims.quote: His most recent victory 6 months ago was against a former UFC guy in Tyson Nam to where he violently uh put him away.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein holds notable wins over opponents like Tyson Nam.
claims.quote: notable wins um over guys like Tyson Nam

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein has 15 wins with 14 coming inside the distance.
claims.quote: this guy has 15 wins. 14 of those wins come inside the distance.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein has a speed edge over Cody Gibson on the feet.
claims.quote: striking wise, little bit of that speed edge over the Cody Gibson side.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein is a young, explosive, and well-rounded talent who outmatches Cody Gibson.
claims.quote: I just think Abdul, he's young, he's explosive, he's wellrounded... I just feel like he's outmatched here by a young, explosive, well-rounded talent in Abdul uh Hussein.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is pretty big for the weight class at 5 foot 10 with a 71 inch reach.
claims.quote: 5 foot 10, 71 in reach. These are strong advantages to have in the bantamweight division.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson will have the height and reach advantage in this matchup.
claims.quote: Gibson's going to be the longer rangier guy.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is five inches taller than Abdul Hussein.
claims.quote: He's tall, too. Five inches taller, which I think is a factor, right?

claims.claim: Cody Gibson holds height and reach advantages over Abdul Hussein.
claims.quote: he is a lot taller than um Abdul Hussein, Cody Gibson, that is... He does have a reach advantage

claims.claim: Cody Gibson could give Hussein trouble in the early moments by coming out hard and fast, pressing him up against the cage.
claims.quote: I think Gibson in the early moments could give Hussein a little bit of trouble. I mean, Cody Gibson round one against Dean Blackshere came out like a bat out of hell. And there's a possibility he tries to do something like that here.

claims.claim: Hussein has a grappling threat that can force Gibson to engage and lead to a submission finish.
claims.quote: possibility of him locking up a sub could end up being like a nice shot from Hussein. Forces Gibson to try enga to engage in the wrestling a bit and then Hussein ends up in dominant position locking in a strangle.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is 38 years old and has questions about how much is left in the tank.
claims.quote: versus Gibson at 38. I don't know how much is left in the tank for a 38-year-old Cody Gibson.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson has consistently struggled with grappling throughout his career.
claims.quote: Cody Gibson, who has really struggled with the grappling throughout the years.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson's chin is questionable following his 21-second knockout loss to Aoriqileng.
claims.quote: Gibson's chin don't know where it's at because of his last loss where he got knocked out by our kilong in 20 seconds.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson was brutally starched early in the first round by Richie Loung roughly eight months ago.
claims.quote: getting brutally starched early in the fight by a Richie Long um in in the very first round eight months ago.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is susceptible to being put away with a big shot or troubled on the ground when pressured on his heels.
claims.quote: we've seen that if you can get Cody Gibson, you know, on his heels, you can land a big shot and put him away, but you can also take him down and give him some problems on the ground

claims.claim: Cody Gibson lacks the strength at 38 years old to control other bantamweights in the clinch or grappling exchanges.
claims.quote: At 38, I don't think Gibson's all that strong anymore. Again, I try to say that respectfully.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein's only career losses are to Muhammad Mokaev and Nariman Abbasov.
claims.quote: And the only career losses for Abdul Hussein are Mame, sorry, Muhammad Mokayv back in 2021 in Brave and Narwas Abska in Brave in 2018.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein has strong and tricky overall grappling skills.
claims.quote: He's pretty tricky when it comes to the overall grappling though. And I think that's going to be his main path to victory

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein had a competitive fight and successfully took down Muhammad Mokaev in the past.
claims.quote: even though Hussein lost that fight, it was rather competitive at at moments, right? We saw Abdul taking down Muhammad Makay.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein covers distance well and uses light, karate-taekwondo-esque footwork.
claims.quote: he makes up for that by covering distance very well. He's very springy in his stance... Kind of karate taekwond doesque with the light footwork.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein's primary strengths are landing big shots on the feet and taking the fight to the floor.
claims.quote: his bread and butter is landing the big shots on the feet, getting the fight to the floor

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein controls the lead hand well and works off it when fighting in the southpaw stance.
claims.quote: When he stands in the southpaw stance like he did against Tyson Nom, he controls that lead hand real well.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein demonstrates high-level grappling ability by out-grappling a high-level fighter.
claims.quote: you're out grappling a super highle fighter... that speaks volumes to the skill level that Abdul brings to the table.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson lacks the remaining skills and miles left on him to deal with a young, dangerous fighter like Abdul Hussein.
claims.quote: I just don't know that Cody Gibson has the skills at this point and and the miles left on him to be able to deal with a super young dangerous guy in Abdul Hussein.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson will utilize footwork, front kicks, and boxing combinations to keep Hussein off him early.
claims.quote: utilize footwork, front kicks up the middle, and just try to pick at Abdul Hussein where with with some of those technical boxing combos that we see Cody Gibson bring to the table.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein has knockout power in his hands and good combinations.
claims.quote: Some knockout power in his hands and some good combinations. I like the way he rips the body.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein possesses explosive hand speed and real knockout power.
claims.quote: explosive hand speed, real knockout power

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein has insane power and is a really good wrestler and grappler.
claims.quote: this guy's power is insane, but not only that, he's a really good wrestler, and he's a phenomenal grappler as well.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein's grappling was competitive when he fought Muhammad Mokaev.
claims.quote: His grappling was competitive with Mohamed Makaya. He was scoring some takedowns.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson has a good wrestling background and is a veteran in the sport.
claims.quote: good wrestling uh background, and definitely a vet in the game.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is hitable and tends to make mistakes on the ground.
claims.quote: He's always been hitable. He's always made a lot of mistakes in the mat.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson is taking this fight on 16 days notice.
claims.quote: Cody Gibson's taken this one on 16 days noticed.

claims.claim: Abdul Hussein was originally scheduled to fight on the Ultimate Fighter and later booked against Muin Gaffarov before Gaffarov pulled out.
claims.quote: Hussein was scheduled to fight on the Ultimate Fighter... He was then booked against Muin Gaffarov. Gaffroof have to pull out.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson's long limbs pose grappling and length advantages that make him difficult to keep down.
claims.quote: those long limbs can cause problems, pose issues potentially on the mat.

claims.claim: Cody Gibson has a height and reach disadvantage against Abdul Husan.
claims.quote: a bit of a height and reach disadvantage, advantage, whatever you want to call it.
